text: robotic assisted transabdominal preperitoneal inguinal hernia repair with mesh for a giant scrotal hernia by Dr. Andrew Iraniha. This patient is a twenty nine year old gentleman with a long history of a giant right inguinal hernia. On the physical exam, the patient had an extremely large right scrotal inguinal hernia. which was associated with the on and off pain and discomfort, especially during the activity and the straining. This video demonstrate the robotic assisted transabdominal preperitoneal right inguinal hernia repair for a giant right inguinal hernia with mesh.

The surgeon begins the operation by making a transverse incision in the peritoneum from the anterior superior iliac spine towards the medial umbilical cord. The preperitoneal space is accessed, and a preperitoneal flap is created. Dissection proceeds medially towards the symphysis pubis and pubic bone, reducing the large hernia sac from the defect. The hernia site is notably large, with significant attachments to the testicular vessels and vas deferens, requiring careful dissection to avoid injury to these structures.

The surgeon demonstrates the dissection of the vas deferens, which is completely separated from the hernia sac. The vesicular vessels remain attached to the sac, highlighting the need for careful attention to avoid injury to these blood vessels.

The hernia sac is shown completely dissected from the blood vessels, with the vas deferens and testicular vessels isolated and protected. The surgeon dissects inferiorly, superiorly, and laterally to create a pocket for mesh placement. A three-dimensional Marlex mesh is inserted into the pre-peritoneal space, laid over the defect, and secured to the abdominal wall with a bio-loop. The peritoneal defect is closed with a running suture, incorporating the hernia site to strengthen the peritoneum.

This procedure was performed as an outpatient in a surgical center and patient was discharged home the same day. He had a very quick recovery with minimal pain and discomfort after the surgery and was able to resume his regular activity right after the operation. This video demonstrates the application of the robotic platform for repair of giant inguinal hernia. Robotic platform allows the surgeon to perform this operation with precision and better visualization and therefore be able to offer the minimally invasive surgery even in this subset of the patients with the very large giant hernia.
